opp.ais_why:
• Per the 21 Jul 2025 rep note: 'Add info on AI requested (tbr by Aug 01).' Per the 1 Aug 2025 entry: 'AI clarification responded.' This is a verbatim buyer-side AI-clarification request as part of the formal CLM tender ‚Äî MTR's procurement team explicitly requested additional information about Zycus's AI capabilities during the evaluation cycle.
• On 4 May 2026: 'Confirmation project is frozen due to other internal priorities. KIV and get update by July.' On 19 Sep 2024: 'Update from On Wing next week (confirmation if they open a selection process).' MTR is a long-running multi-stage evaluation (~14 months captured) bundling CLM with eAuction/Sourcing. The AI-info clarification was a real, dated, buyer-originated AI question ‚Äî but the project is currently frozen.
• Confidence is Medium. MTR has only Merlin in product scope (single AI SKU bundled with iContract for CLM) and the AI signal is the formal info-request rather than a champion-led AI demand. Project is frozen as of May 2026. Score 6 (boundary DOWN per rubric) reflects genuine buyer AI-clarification ask without an active AI-led evaluation.

items.source: Avoma discovery (opp+account+attendee-email) returned zero matched calls

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. MTR requested a first dollar estimation on 19 May 2025 (Next Step), signalling early budget scoping, but no ROI, savings, cycle-time, headcount, or compliance-cost figure has been developed or validated with the buyer. The Next Step history frames the initiative qualitatively: migrating some Oracle EBS procurement modules and a parallel GenAI use case (03 Mar 2025). Without a quantified value case the tender is scored on functional fit versus Icertis rather than financial return, which is a weakness given EY's Icertis lean. Building a quantified ROI model tied to MTR's contract volume and GenAI mandate is the single highest-leverage differentiation move.

sources: Contact Role: Navjot Singh, Procurement Transformation
champion.narrative: No champion is identified. The record explicitly states 'not able to identify champion / exec sponsor engagement' (08 Dec 2025) and attempts to secure exec engagement via EY (12 Sep, 22 Sep 2025) never confirmed. Navjot Singh (Procurement Head - Centre of Excellence, Procurement Transformation) is the strongest candidate because his transformation mandate aligns with the Merlin AI story, but there is no evidence of advocacy, introductions, or pushing back on EY's Icertis lean. Lilian Au-Yeung is a helpful and responsive tender gatekeeper (active Jun 2026) but is an administrator, not a decision influencer. Converting Navjot Singh into an internal advocate for the Merlin/GenAI differentiation, before commercials, is the developing-champion move.

competition.narrative: Zycus is in a competitive public tender against Icertis (primary, EY-favoured) and Sirion Labs. Competitors__c lists 'iCertis; Sirion Labs', and the Next Step of 05 Nov 2025 confirms 'EY (supporting process) seems to push for Icertis (working with Alliance to mitigate)', a structural disadvantage because the advisor shapes and scores the tender. The Zycus wedge is Merlin (GenAI contract intelligence) tied to MTR's stated GenAI mandate and the scored AI-clarification requirement; Icertis is comparatively rules-based CLM. How we win: make Merlin the strategic centrepiece of the tender response, quantify the AI/automation ROI, and reach the budget owner so the AI case is evaluated as strategic value rather than a feature checkbox, neutralising EY's Icertis lean.

sources: Task 20 Mar 2026 (Simon Fung, risk / KYC platform need)
identify_pain.narrative: MTR's pain is articulated qualitatively but never quantified. Two threads are consistent across the record: legacy Oracle EBS procurement modules being migrated and a stated mandate to adopt GenAI in procurement ('they want/must start with GenAI use case', 03 Mar 2025), plus a parallel Supply Chain Risk Management / KYC framework need (20 Mar 2026, Simon Fung). The bundling of eAuction and CLM (09 Jun 2025) suggests a broader desire to consolidate procurement tooling. The pain is real enough to drive a formal tender, but no operational metric (contract cycle time, legal-review bottleneck, compliance exposure, cost leakage) and no single pain owner have been captured, which limits the strength of the value story versus Icertis.

sources: Task 20 Mar 2026 (3rd Party Risk Management / KYC)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting will follow MTR's public-tender framework: submissions run through the www.hkextender.com e-tender portal (registration approved 30 Jun 2026 after an initial rejection on 28 Jun), the mutual NDA was executed on 15 Jun 2026, and a third-party risk-management / KYC requirement is in scope (20 Mar 2026 email from Simon Fung). Formal MSA/SOW redlining, InfoSec review, and any systems-integration deep-dive (Oracle EBS) will follow vendor selection. There is no Current_Contract_Expiration__c populated, so there is no contractual renewal forcing function; the tender timeline itself is the clock. What would create visibility is MTR's published tender schedule and evaluation milestones, which should be extracted from the Q114354 document.

sources: Contact Role: Alex Gautier, Decision Maker
Task 14 Jun 2026 Horizon invite to Alex
No Economic Buyer contact role present
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is engaged. Alex Gautier (General Manager - Procurement & Supply Chain) is the Salesforce Decision Maker and the most senior mapped contact, but there is no record of him joining a call or commercial conversation; outreach to him was limited to Horizon-event invitations (25 Mar and 14 Jun 2026 emails). No budget owner or CFO-level sponsor is named in the contact roles, and the AIS note flags 'not able to identify champion / exec sponsor engagement' (08 Dec 2025). In a public tender the formal budget sits above procurement, so identifying and reaching the budget owner behind Q114354 is a ranked move. What the evidence does show is that procurement (Alex Gautier, Navjot Singh, Lilian Au-Yeung) owns the process today, not that a commercial sponsor has been secured.

sources: Task 15 Jun 2026 (Lilian confirms Carl as point of contact)
decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, advisor-supported public procurement process, which is well mapped even without call transcripts. MTR shortlisted Zycus on 07 Jul 2025 (partnering with EY per the 12 Jan 2026 Next Step), ran multiple clarification rounds through 2025, froze the project on 04 May 2026 for internal priorities, then issued the formal CLMS tender Q114354 on 09 Jun 2026 with a 16 Jun briefing via the hkextender e-tender portal. Lilian Au-Yeung (Assistant Manager - Procurement & Contracts) administers the tender and confirmed Carl Kimball as Zycus's single point of contact (15 Jun 2026). The approval chain above procurement is not documented and no decision date has been committed post-tender; securing the evaluation timeline and shortlist decision date in writing is the open item.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 09 Jun 2025 (RFI), 21 Jul 2025 (AI clarification), 01 Sep 2025, 03 Oct 2025
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are being formalised through the public tender rather than a discovery call, so they are partly visible. MTR ran an RFI for CLM (received 09 Jun 2025, responded 12 Jun 2025), requested AI-capability clarification as a scored element (21 Jul 2025), sought clarification on implementation and maintenance (01 Sep 2025) and on configuration (03 Oct 2025), and raised a Q&A on contract storage size during the 2026 tender. These signal that AI/GenAI capability, configuration flexibility, implementation and maintenance model, and data/storage capacity are weighted criteria. The full scored requirement set now lives in the Q114354 tender document, which Zycus received on 15 Jun 2026; extracting the scoring rubric and weightings from it is the way to confirm the criteria precisely.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 41.

EY advisor supports and scores the tender and favours Icertis; Zycus counter via partner Alliance is unproven.
No quantified ROI or value case; buyer asked for a first dollar estimate but none was validated.
Close date 18 Dec 2026 is optimistic for a full post-tender motion; project already froze once (04 May 2026) and CloseDate slipped 26 Jun -> 18 Dec.

cro_panel.header: MTR Corporation Limited — Shortlisted · $260K · closes 18 Dec

contributions.evidence: Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 41 is carried by competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15); commercial strength +0.40 (weight 5). Offsetting it: momentum 51 vs stage-expected 56.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 51 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 12 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 22 is anchored on the stage win (41), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 51, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Verdict flags close-date risk this sweep.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 (Moderate). MTR made a formal, dated AI-clarification request during the tender (21 Jul 2025, responded 01 Aug 2025) and stated a GenAI mandate (03 Mar 2025), so there is genuine buyer AI appetite, but it is procurement-driven due diligence rather than a champion-led AI demand, and no call exists to confirm depth.

deal_movement.summary: The deal came back to life in June 2026: a May freeze lifted when MTR issued the formal CLMS tender Q114354 (09 Jun), Zycus executed the NDA, attended the 16 Jun briefing, and completed e-tender portal registration; Carl Kimball (VP APJ) took ownership. Engagement is currently rep-driven tender logistics, not a buyer value conversation.

critical_signals.text: MTR's tender advisor EY has been steering toward Icertis since November 2025, and our partner-led counter is still unproven.
MTR issued the formal CLMS tender Q114354 on 9 June 2026, lifting the May freeze and putting the deal back into active evaluation.
Zycus executed the NDA and attended the 16 June tender briefing, but every June touch was tender logistics, not a buyer value conversation.
Lilian Au-Yeung is our only actively engaged contact as tender gatekeeper, while the decision maker and budget owner remain unengaged.
There is no quantified ROI and no identified budget owner, so the $260k proposal cannot yet be justified to anyone who signs.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement rebounded in June 2026 with the live tender, but it is rep-driven tender logistics; no buyer value conversation or senior sponsorship has been secured, so momentum is process activity, not deal conviction.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 13 Jun 2025, qualified same day; Shortlisted since 07 Jul 2025 (about 361 days in stage). Close 18 Dec 2026 is 168 days out. Project froze 04 May 2026, then the formal CLMS tender issued 09 Jun 2026 and Zycus is now actively responding (NDA executed 15 Jun, briefing 16 Jun, portal registration approved 30 Jun). Post-tender enterprise motion: evaluation and vendor selection 60-90 days, commercials and ROI 30-45 days, InfoSec and legal 30-45 days, contracting 30 days = 135-180 days minimum from award. With submission still closing in early July, the 18 Dec date is only feasible on an uninterrupted, compressed path; any slip or a further internal freeze breaks it. CloseDate already pushed once (26 Jun -> 18 Dec, on 30 Apr 2026).
evidence: Formal CLMS tender Q114354 issued 09 Jun 2026; Zycus registered and responding (last activity 30 Jun 2026)
NDA executed 15 Jun 2026, tender briefing attended 16 Jun 2026
Shortlisted stage held about 361 days (since 07 Jul 2025)
CloseDate pushed 26 Jun -> 18 Dec 2026 on 30 Apr 2026 (cumulative slip from original Q3 2025 target)
No economic buyer engaged; Alex Gautier (Decision Maker) never on a recorded call
No champion identified; exec-sponsor engagement failed (Next Step 08 Dec 2025)
EY advisor pushing Icertis (Next Step 05 Nov 2025); mitigation via partner Alliance unproven
Zero buyer calls discovered in the trailing 13 months; Best Case forecast is indefensible
north_star_verdict.headline: Frozen in May, the deal is live again: MTR issued the formal CLMS tender (Q114354) on 09 Jun 2026, Zycus executed the NDA and attended the 16 Jun briefing. But the 18 Dec close is optimistic for a full public-tender motion, no economic buyer or champion is engaged, and EY is steering MTR toward Icertis.

flags: Neutralise EY's Icertis lean: make Merlin GenAI the strategic centrepiece of the Q114354 response, not a feature checkbox.
Break single-threading: only Lilian Au-Yeung (tender admin) is engaged; secure Navjot Singh as a transformation champion and reach the budget owner behind the tender.
Quantify the value case: build an ROI model on MTR's contract volume and Oracle EBS migration so procurement can defend Zycus to Finance.
best_practice_check.summary: Zycus is live in a formal public tender against an EY-favoured Icertis, but with no economic buyer, no champion, and no quantified value case. The win levers are reaching the budget owner, converting a transformation-lead champion, and making the tender response a Merlin/GenAI ROI story rather than a feature match.

competitive_position.summary: Icertis is the single strongest current threat, favoured by MTR's advisor EY who 'seems to push for Icertis' (05 Nov 2025); Zycus is countering via partner Alliance but the outcome is unproven. Sirion Labs is named in the tender field with no active buyer signal (low threat). The award is being decided through the formal Q114354 public tender, so no competitor is buyer-confirmed as preferred; EY's influence is the risk to watch. Zycus's wedge is the Merlin GenAI story against MTR's stated AI mandate.

Zycus is aligned on AI and CLM functional fit (evidenced by the shortlist) but exposed on quantified value and on integration proof.
